name: SimplE
description: SimplE is a simple enhancement of Canonical Polyadic (CP) decomposition designed for link prediction, addressing CP's weakness where it learns two independent embedding vectors for each entity when they should be tied. This modification allows the two embeddings of each entity to be learned dependently, and its complexity grows linearly with the size of the embeddings. The embeddings derived from SimplE are interpretable, allowing certain types of background knowledge to be incorporated via weight tying. Theoretically, it is proven to be fully expressive, and a bound on the required embedding size for full expressivity is derived. Empirically, SimplE outperforms several state-of-the-art tensor factorization techniques despite its simplicity. The code for SimplE can be found on GitHub at https://github.com/Mehran-k/SimplE.
